speaker: Simo Belmar (guest lecturer, standing in for Tom Eagar)
speaker_note: Not delivered by Tom Eagar. Belmar is a former MIT PhD (graduated 2006) and consultant who collaborates with Eagar on projects. Self-identifies in opening paragraph.
topics_covered: Definition of safety factor / factor of safety
Frozen copper pipe case (hoop stress failure)
Welded joint strength reduction in pressure vessels
New Orleans levee / Katrina (soil shear properties)
Automotive fail-safe design and weight cost
Speedboat propeller shaft fatigue failure (Inconel, 400-hour design life)
cases_introduced: New Orleans levee failure during Katrina
Jet ski molded nylon fuel-tank fitting
board_drawings: Cylindrical pressure vessel with longitudinal seam
Speedboat shaft and propeller (water-level cross-section, fracture surface with beach marks)
references_to_eagar: p3: 'Professor Eagar talked about the pressure vessel code'
p7: 'or you may have Tom Eagar' (next-day uncertainty)
p9: Eagar's spring lecture on forming
p21: 'doubler plate, like Professor Eagar mentioned yesterday'
p25: 'I'm sure Professor [Eagar] started to introduce to you some of these values'
p32: 'that's a number from Professor Eagar, I think it is material selection module'
p55: 'Professor Eagar, I know last class, mentioned about service history'
constitutional_note: This lecture is part of the Eagar corpus by virtue of being delivered in Eagar's course during Eagar's absence. Editorial treatment matches the Eagar pipeline conventions. Cases introduced here are Belmar's professional cases, not Eagar's teaching cases, and should be tagged accordingly in the aggregate case index.
